methodology: Platform-product OKRs with a small set of company objectives, outcome-oriented key results, and explicit team ownership across platform product areas.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe platform outcomes for internal customers, not internal feature checklists.
Key results focus on adoption, flow, controls, and reliability rather than tool rollout volume.
Every key result assigns executing and supporting teams so shared ownership stays concrete.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make the platform the easiest path for mainstream teams
companyObjectives.statement: Improve onboarding, discoverability, and paved-road quality so stream-aligned teams choose the platform because it reduces real work.

inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows that platform adoption depends on product-quality onboarding and visible reduction of setup work.

keyResults.target: Bring mainstream team onboarding onto a two-day path
keyResults.baseline: New team onboarding still depends on manual support and hidden platform knowledge
keyResults.statement: Reduce team onboarding time by improving portal workflows, golden-path coverage, and self-service discovery.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Make the platform the easiest path for mainstream teams

executingTeams.intent: Lead the workflow and discoverability improvements that reduce onboarding friction.

supportingTeams.intent: Expand the range and quality of supported starting points so teams need less custom setup.
Capture migration friction and feed it back into platform product changes.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: Outcome should be pursued through iterative workflow and product improvements.

keyResults.metric: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
keyResults.target: Make the first supported production deployment fast enough to prove the paved road works
keyResults.baseline: Teams still spend too much time wiring runtime, delivery, and platform dependencies together
keyResults.statement: Reduce time to first production deployment by aligning templates, pipelines, environment provisioning, and runtime patterns.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Make the platform the easiest path for mainstream teams

executingTeams.intent: Own the shared rollout path and release automation required for first production success.

supportingTeams.intent: Provide the runtime provisioning path that product teams can use without ticket queues.
Clarify the supported dependency and runtime access patterns.
Package the supported path into usable templates.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: Requires coordinated improvements across multiple platform product areas.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make the standard path safer and more operable by default
companyObjectives.statement: Increase platform-wide control coverage, telemetry consistency, and incident response quality without pushing teams back into manual governance queues.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Trusted supply chain baseline: validated discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows teams accept stronger controls when those controls are built into the default path.
Shapes the exception model required to keep controls usable.

keyResults.target: Push baseline identity, artifact, and policy coverage into the normal delivery path
keyResults.baseline: Control adoption varies significantly across product teams and legacy services
keyResults.statement: Increase baseline control coverage by integrating identity, policy, and trusted artifact standards into templates and pipelines.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Make the standard path safer and more operable by default

executingTeams.intent: Lead the control-baseline improvements and exception workflow design.

supportingTeams.intent: Embed required checks into the delivery path so teams inherit them automatically.
Make control-compliant patterns part of the default starting point.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: The exact combination of policy, artifact, and identity changes should follow measured adoption and incident evidence.

keyResults.metric: Incident MTTR for common platform failure classes
keyResults.target: Shorten response and recovery time for recurring shared-platform incidents
keyResults.baseline: Incident response remains inconsistent because ownership, telemetry, and runbook quality vary
keyResults.statement: Reduce MTTR by improving telemetry defaults, routing, and incident automation for shared platform failures.
keyResults.companyObjectiveTitle: Make the standard path safer and more operable by default

executingTeams.intent: Own the telemetry and response patterns that reduce shared-platform incident duration.

supportingTeams.intent: Improve ownership visibility and service metadata used during incidents.
Quantify failure classes and alert-quality patterns that deserve automation.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: Needs iterative improvement of alert quality, ownership data, and automation, not a single feature drop.
